# Learner Mobile — Platform Summary (llms.txt)

## 1. Overview
Learner Mobile is a mobile-first, cloud-native learning platform with a software-with-service approach. We combine a powerful, built-in Course Creator, AI-assisted content generation (AI Source-to-Course™), robust reporting and integrations, and a learner-centered mobile experience. Our platform supports both formal compliance training and informal, on-the-job microlearning, and we provide onboarding, instructional design, and ongoing client success services.

## 2. Key Capabilities
### Authoring & Content
- Built-in Course Creator: full authoring tool for interactive modules (hotspots, flip cards, carousels, quizzes).
- AI Source-to-Course™: convert documents (PDF, DOCX, PPT) or prompts into multi-lesson courses with quizzes and images.
- Supported export/import: SCORM 1.2 (import/export), xAPI (import/export), SCORM 2004 4th Ed (export), PDF export.
- Media support: video uploads (recommend <500 MB), audio, images, integrated Canva support, in-platform image editor.
- Pre-built library & templates: editable library courses for quick customization.

### Learner Experience
- Native mobile apps: iOS (iPhone/iPad) and Android (phones/tablets) plus responsive web.
- Offline access: download courses, complete offline; progress syncs when reconnected.
- Progress resume and autosave: automatic save after each page or interaction; resume across devices.
- Profiles & Channels: learners belong to profiles (role, location, department); channels organize content per audience.
- Search & discovery: title/keyword/metadata search, filters, To-Do List for required items.
- Achievements: badges and certificates (downloadable PDFs, share to LinkedIn where enabled).

### Assessments & Policies
- Quizzes: multiple choice, multi-select, true/false, short answer; AI can auto-generate quizzes from content.
- Passing scores & attempt limits: configurable per quiz (passing thresholds, retry rules, time limits, attempt tracking).
- Feedback: configurable immediate feedback, per-question feedback, attempt history (best vs. most recent score configurable).

### Instructor-Led & On-the-Job
- ILT workflows: scheduling, rostering, real-time roster views, attendance tracking (enhancements planned for QR/badge scanning).
- Ad hoc/on-the-job: supports manager sign-off and informal training capture (planned/enhanced features).
- Integration of live links: embed Teams/Zoom links into onboarding modules.

### Reporting & Analytics
- Role-based reporting and dashboards; configurability by admin role.
- Report types: adoption, compliance, engagement, readiness, achievements; exportable CSV.
- Power BI / Tableau integration: direct access to Azure data lakes for advanced visualizations and automated dashboards.
- Automated notifications & digest emails: scheduled reminders for assignments and past-due training.

### Integrations & Security
- OpenAPI platform, RESTful APIs, SSO support (SAML 2.0, OIDC), AD integration, LDAP compatibility.
- Integrations: Microsoft ecosystem (Teams, Power BI), Canva, Synthesia, Workday, Zapier and other external content providers (links/SSO).
- Enterprise-grade hosting on Microsoft Azure, monitoring via Datadog, ServiceNow integration for incident management.

## 3. Admin Experience & Content Management
### Content Hub & Channel Management
- Content screen: list and card/channel views; filter by profile, search, status.
- Channel view: drag-and-drop card management, create channels, pin featured cards.
- Channel settings: name, description, image edit (crop, annotate, filters), sort order (Newest, Most Viewed, Top Rated, A–Z).
- Content list view: sortable columns (title, type, channels, published date, author), hover previews, draft/published states.

### Course Creation Flows
- Create options: Start from scratch, Edit library course, AI Source-to-Course, Upload eLearning (SCORM/xAPI), Build curriculum.
- Curriculum builder: combine courses into sequential learning paths; track across curricula.
- Pre-publish preview: preview in desktop/tablet/mobile modes.
- Save as Draft vs Publish: drafts hide content from learners until published.

### Course Creator Elements & Properties
- Element types: text, images, audio, video, two-column layouts, callouts, knowledge checks, open response, image+text overlays.
- Element controls: paddings, background color, width, position, mobile stacking toggles, feedback fields for quiz elements.
- Image editor: crop, filters, finetune (brightness, contrast, saturation), annotate, stickers, redact, resize; undo/redo.

### Content QA & Best Practices
- Test SCORM files in external player before upload.
- Use profile filter for auditing audience visibility.
- Use drafts for staging before publish.
- Use AI for prompt-driven content and quiz generation, but review outputs for instructional quality.

## 4. Learner-Facing Guides & Policies
- Login options: username/password or SSO (organization dependent).
- Navigation: left sidebar for lessons, top search bar, main content area with interactive elements.
- To-Do List: required assignments with due dates; digest emails and push notifications for new/overdue items.
- Comments & feedback: per-lesson comments, star ratings, admin-visible feedback channels.
- Certificates & sharing: downloadable certificates; LinkedIn share (controlled by org settings).

## 5. Reporting Use Cases & Examples
- Compliance reporting: who has/hasn't completed required training; filter by learner, course, or group.
- Readiness: certifications and mastery reporting to track time-to-productivity.
- Engagement: track optional content usage, searches, ratings to inform content strategy.
- Adoption: month-over-month active user tracking and time-in-training metrics.

## 6. Enterprise Services & Support
- Onboarding: guided setup, migrations, admin and content creator training.
- Client Success: dedicated client success manager, instructional design services, content production support.
- Support: email/phone support during business hours; 24/7 help desk options for enterprise clients.
- Incident management: Datadog-based monitoring, ServiceNow integration, runbook triage, rollback support via Azure staging slots.

## 7. Roadmap & AI Capabilities
- Current AI features: document-to-course conversion, automated quiz generation, translations (100+ languages), grammar/readability suggestions, image generation, integrations with Synthesia and Canva.
- Planned enhancements: predictive analytics, enhanced search, AI-generated video content, ILT attendance capture improvements (QR and badge scanning), advanced gamification (leaderboards).

## 8. Security, Compliance & Ownership
- Identity & provisioning: SSO, AD integration, scheduled/JIT provisioning.
- Data integration: REST APIs, OpenAPI, Azure data lake access for visualizations.
- Content ownership: custom-developed training modules become client-owned (per engagement terms).
- Enterprise controls: configurable roles, permissions, and data access based on organizational structure.

## 9. Admin Quick Actions (how-to highlights)
- Create new course: Content → + Create New → choose method (Start from Scratch / AI / SCORM / Library / Curriculum).
- Add quiz: Lesson Map → Add Lesson → Add Quiz or Generate Quiz (AI).
- Set passing score: Quiz Introduction Screen → Properties → Passing Score and retake rules.
- Preview course: Click Preview → choose Desktop / Tablet / Mobile view.
- Assign courses: Course → Assignments → choose learners or profiles, set due dates/rules.
- Add admins: Users → Create Admin → assign roles/profiles/permissions.

## 10. Best Practices & Recommendations
- Start with library content when possible to accelerate delivery and ensure quality.
- Use profiles and channels to keep content targeted and manageable.
- Use drafts and preview frequently to catch layout/pacing issues across devices.
- Use AI Source-to-Course to accelerate creation, then refine with instructional design review.
- Integrate reporting with Power BI for organization-level dashboards and executive insights.

## 11. Public Site & Platform Links

- **Training Platform (Homepage)** — `https://training.learnermobile.com/`  
  The primary landing page for Learner Mobile’s training product. Promotes mobile-first access ("Access Anywhere, Anytime"), offline mode, the AI Source-to-Course feature, ease of course creation, and scalability. Includes calls to action like “Book a demo” and “Try it for free”. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

- **Pricing Page** — `https://training.learnermobile.com/pricing`  
  Presents Learner Mobile’s pricing options for different user or organizational tiers. Designed to help prospective customers understand cost vs value as they scale. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

- **Contact Us** — `https://training.learnermobile.com/contact-us`  
  A “Let’s Talk Training” page. Invites potential customers/partners to reach out with their goals, e.g. faster course creation or smarter delivery. Used for lead capture. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

- **Login Portal** — `https://login.learnermobile.com`  
  The user login page. For existing users or administrators to access their Learner Mobile accounts. Includes options such as “Forgot password?”. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

- **Free Trial / Sign-Up (Admin)** — `https://admin.learnermobile.com/Account/SignUp/`  
  Sign up page for new users to begin a free trial or administrative access. Enables account creation / onboarding. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}
